source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage/Crane Other Lifting

actions_taken: Medic examined the contract employee on April 21, 2018
Physician instructed to administer Ibuprofen and ice pack
Contract employee transported to medical facility on April 27, 2018
MRI performed to diagnose labrum fracture
event.summary: On April 21, 2018 at 0900 hours at platform KC 875A (Lucius), a contract employee injured his right shoulder while attempting to carry two 20 lb scaffold boards approximately 7 ft. long. The employee repositioned to avoid a cable crossing the walkway and felt his shoulder pop. Initial medical examination found no concerns and the employee was cleared to return to full duty the following day. On April 27, 2018 at 0300 hours, the employee felt his shoulder popping out of place while in his bunk and was transported to a medical facility where an MRI revealed a labrum fracture.
recommendations: Contract employee should have requested help with carrying the two scaffold boards to alleviate the need to reposition while carrying

threats.name: Improper lifting technique due to obstacle avoidance
Failure to request assistance for manual handling task

controls.name: Request assistance for manual handling of heavy objects
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to prevent improper lifting
controls.lod_basis: Primary prevention measure to avoid unsafe lifting practices

supporting_text: The CE should have requested help with carrying the two scaffold boards. This may have alleviated the CE's attempt to reposition while carrying the boards.

controls.name: Clear and maintain unobstructed walkways
controls.barrier_role: Engineering/administrative control to prevent hazardous conditions
controls.lod_basis: Primary prevention to eliminate walkway obstructions

supporting_text: he noticed a cable several feet high that crossed the walkway being used as an anchor for a welding enclosure

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
